entries.title: w40k data update: 886 to 895
entries.summary: Balance pass. Veiled Blade assassin points up, 13 new Space Marines / Black Templars leader groups, Tyranids Frame keyword, one new core rule, 2 new FAQs.
entries.html:
Date swapped: 2026-07-15. Source: official Warhammer 40,000 app base.apk (pulled 2026-07-15), exported with scripts/w40k_exporter/w40k_exporter.py per CODEX_ARMORUM_DATA_UPDATE.md. This file records every change between data_version 886 and data_version 895, derived from a field-level diff of the old and new w40k.db (summary compare via scripts/compare_w40k_db.py, then a full-table row diff; no table outside the sections below changed).

Headline: a small balance pass. No schema changes, no factions added or removed, no datasheets added or removed, no detachment/battle-size/keyword changes. Everything below is content-level.

1. Points changes (4 datasheets)§

Only the Veiled Blade Elimination Force detachment tier moved; the base Agents of the Imperium tier and the untagged (allied) tier are unchanged.

Datasheet [Imperial Agents]Tier886895
Callidus AssassinVeiled Blade Elimination Force100140
Culexus AssassinVeiled Blade Elimination Force85125
Eversor AssassinVeiled Blade Elimination Force110145
Vindicare AssassinVeiled Blade Elimination Force110155

Unchanged for reference: Callidus 100/100, Culexus 85/85, Eversor 110/120, Vindicare 110/125 (Agents tier / allied tier).

2. Leader attachments (leader_group 1267 to 1280, +13 groups)§

All 13 new groups are Space Marines / Black Templars ecosystem:

  • The Black Templars' own Sternguard Veteran Squad datasheet (cec275e8-0bf3-499c-9646-3b84443eb02c) is now attachable for the generic Space Marines characters Ancient, Apothecary, Captain, Chaplain, Judiciar, Lieutenant (6 groups). Previously only Castellan, Emperor's Champion and Marshal could lead it. Each affected character's leads_units now lists "Sternguard Veteran Squad" twice (the Space Marines datasheet and the Black Templars datasheet share a name).
  • Castellan, Emperor's Champion, Marshal each gained a duplicate second group for that same Black Templars Sternguard datasheet (3 groups). This is redundant data in the source app, not a rules change.
  • Eradicator Squad with Heavy Bolters is now leadable at all: new groups for Apothecary Biologis, Captain in Gravis Armour, Iron Father Feirros, Tor Garadon (4 groups). Its can_be_led_by went from empty to those four.

3. Other datasheet content changes§

  • Amonhotekh's Guard Canoptek Doomstalker [Necrons]: lost its Reanimation Protocols faction ability row (this is the ability table's net -1, 4310 to 4309).
  • Kairos Fateweaver [Scintillating Legions datasheet 4d987664-7ac1-448b-9cd4-715be7646fd3 only]: Infernal Gateway focused witchfire profile attacks changed D3+6 to D6+6; profile names recapitalised ("focused witchfire" to "Focused Witchfire", "witchfire" to "Witchfire"). The source wargear_loadout.default_loadout array was also emptied (options and choose_from unchanged; our engine still derives a legal default, engine invariants pass). Note: his second datasheet under Legiones Daemonica (02f35395) was NOT updated and still carries D3+6; that inconsistency is in the official app's own data, verified in both the db and our rendered API.
  • Toxicrene, Tyrannocyte, Tyrannofex [Tyranids]: gained the Frame keyword (unit and model keyword lists). This bakes in what the existing "The following units have the FRAME keyword" errata FAQ already said.

4. Stratagem id rotation (no content change)§

The five "renames" the summary compare reports are the same five stratagems with their database ids rotated; the set of (name, rules text) pairs is identical in 886 and 895:

  • Leagues of Votann, Armoured Trailblazers: ids 1124/1125/1126 rotated between Built To Last, Coordinated Crossfire, Outflanking Armour.
  • Necrons, Hand of the Dynasty: ids 1344/1345 swapped between Nanosaturation and Will of the Conqueror.

Nothing in Codex Armorum references stratagem row ids, so this is a no-op.

5. Enforcement-data changes (army-builder relevant)§

  • allied_faction.allowed_warlord_miniatures emptied on all three rows that carried data: Agents of the Imperium (Imperium hosts row), Agents of the Imperium (Deathwatch host row), Heretic Astartes in Legiones Daemonica. Each went from a 8-10 miniature whitelist to []. The builder does not yet consume this field (ally warlord rules are an open parity-gap item), so no behaviour change today; noted for when that lands.
  • enhancement.grants_leader_attachment emptied for Murdermind (Necrons, Hypercrypt/Destroyer Cult; previously granted attachment to Lokhust Destroyers, Lokhust Heavy Destroyers and others) and Exemplar of Duty (Upgrade) (Astra Militarum; previously Ogryn Squad, Bullgryn Squad). Grant groups drop 19 to 13; the engine loads and enforces this data, so those bearers no longer gain extra attach targets, matching the official app.
  • allied_faction.datasheets lists re-ordered on the same three rows (same membership, order only).

6. Core rules, FAQs, missions§

  • New rule 09.05.01 under Normal Move: "A unit cannot make more than one Normal Move in a phase." Merged into data/rules/wh40k_core_rules_combined.md and rebuilt.
  • New rule section "Deployment Card Key" (rule_section 33 to 34, rule_block +20 with 09.05.01): an icon legend for mission deployment cards. Eight icons (CDN-hosted, not exported as files) with captions defining Attacker's/Defender's Battlefield Edge, Deployment Zone and Territory, No Man's Land, and the Home/Central/Expansion Objective icons. Not merged into /rules (it is a mission-card legend keyed to images the export does not carry; the /missions page already renders the layouts themselves). Text recorded here so nothing is lost.
  • Masters of Shadow (Shadowmark Talon detachment) rule text errata'd. Old: "... the target has the Benefit of Cover against that attack." New: "... the target has the Benefit of Cover." The matching FAQ errata entry was replaced: the old FAQ's wording additionally imposed "subtract 1 from the hit roll", which the new FAQ drops entirely.
  • New FAQs (+2 net: 3 added, 1 replaced-out): "The Grenade Stratagem is the same as the Explosives stratagem"; "The Tank Shock Stratagem is the same as the Crushing Impact stratagem"; plus the Shadowmark Talon replacement above.
  • FAQ references (946 to 957, +11): Hive Crone newly linked to the "units lose the Hover core ability" errata; duplicate reference rows added for the existing Tyranids errata (Harpy, Harridan, Hierophant, Hive Crone, Toxicrene, Tyrannocyte, Tyrannofex each now reference their errata twice; duplicate rows in the source app, harmless). FAQ display is driven straight from w40k.db, so these flow through with no manual step.
  • Outflank secondary objective (Chapter Approved pack): typo fix in the 5VP criteria, "is not within 5VP your territory" to "is not within your territory".

7. Verification (all run 2026-07-15, post-swap)§

  • find_datasheet_gaps.py --verify: CHECK A 0 dangling refs; CHECK B gaps 442 <= baseline 442.
  • tests/run_all.py: ALL LAYERS PASS (engine invariants 42, wargear reachability 2, api round-trip 13, multi-detachment 14, golden master 42, fuzz 4). Golden masters were byte-identical, so no re-bless was needed: the snapshots do not cover detachment-gated points tiers or leader-group composition, which is where all the movement in this update happened.
  • Weapon-keyword glossary: no keyword or weapon-ability changes in the diff, so static/weapon_keywords.json untouched.

8. Integration actions taken§

  1. Baseline check, w40k.db.prev backup, export to staging, compare.
  2. File swap: w40k.db, README.md, manifest.json, _reference/ from staging over data/w40k/.
  3. Merged 09.05.01 into the combined rules markdown, build_rules.py rerun.
  4. This record; migration-history entry in CODEX_ARMORUM_ARCHITECTURE.md.

entries.title: Exporter v3: full army-building enforcement data (data_version 886)
entries.summary: The export gained every army-building enforcement structure the app holds: leader groups, warlord and enhancement flags, gated points tiers, keyword limits. The update runbook and compare tool arrived with it.
entries.html:
Third exporter revision, run against the same base.apk (data_version: 886). The on-disk w40k.db was first verified to reproduce byte-identically from the APK, then the exporter was extended strictly additively (structural diff confirmed: every pre-existing table, column and JSON key unchanged).

New tables§

  • datasheet_faction_excluded: all 23 exclusion rows (22 bar parent-faction generics from chapters and cannot be expressed via datasheet_faction).
  • leader_group (1267 rows): bodyguard type (leader/support), required and excluded detachment, requires_all_units_keyword, keyword-based members.
  • detachment_faction_points_cost: per-faction detachment-points overrides.
  • keyword_limit_group and keyword_limit_group_detachment: roster keyword limits and their detachment gates.
  • keyword_ally_restriction.

New columns and JSON keys§

  • model: Warlord and enhancement flags, melee/ranged invulnerable saves.
  • enhancement: take_limit, counts_toward_limit, epic_hero_eligible, non_character_eligible, cannot_be_warlord, grants_leader_attachment.
  • detachment: linked_datasheets (is_warlord, count), unique_keywords, mandatory and granted warlord miniatures.
  • stratagem: secondary-effect CP fields.
  • allied_faction: warlord columns.
  • Points tiers carry required_faction_keyword(_id)s and required_detachment(_id)s (gated pricing); wargear_loadout gained default_loadout (per-miniature equipped-with, including counts); conditional_keywords carry structured requires.

Process tooling added alongside§

  • CODEX_ARMORUM_DATA_UPDATE.md: the top-to-bottom refresh runbook (baseline, export to staging, compare, swap, verify, record).
  • scripts/compare_w40k_db.py: old-vs-new database compare whose report sections map one-to-one onto runbook actions.

The army builder was subsequently rebuilt to consume and enforce most of this data; enforcement status is tracked in docs/WARGEAR_REACHABILITY_PLAN.md and the army-builder parity notes.

entries.title: Re-export: faction exclusions and allied factions (data_version 886)
entries.summary: The exporter learned faction-keyword exclusions (Sir Hekhtur leaves Imperial Knights) and started exporting the allied-faction system.
entries.html:
Second exporter revision, run against the same base.apk (data_version: 886, so the same underlying app data). Two changes to what the export captures:

What changed§

  • **datasheet_faction honours faction_keyword_excluded_datasheet**: bad memberships the app itself suppresses (such as Sir Hekhtur under Imperial Knights) no longer leak into the export. The 1142-datasheet total was unchanged; data_store.py inherited the correction automatically because membership runs only through datasheet_faction. A unit left with zero memberships is skipped at load unless a PRIMARY_FACTION_OVERRIDES rule reassigns it.
  • Allied factions exported: new tables allied_faction, allied_faction_host, allied_faction_datasheet (21 / 87 / 320 rows) plus _reference/allied_factions.{json,csv}. Present but unread by the app at the time; the army builder consumes them now.

Because data_version did not change, the presence of the allied tables, not the version number, signals this newer export.

entries.title: Ruleset source: Wahapedia to the official app database (data_version 886)
entries.summary: Rules data moved to a read-only SQLite export of the official Warhammer 40,000 app. UUID ids, chapters as first-class factions, MFM overlay retired.
entries.html:
The Wahapedia CSV import (catalogue_* tables) and the Munitorum Field Manual points overlay (mfm_* tables) were replaced by data/w40k/w40k.db, a read-only SQLite export of the official Warhammer 40,000 mobile app's own rules database, produced from the user's copy of the app's base.apk by scripts/w40k_exporter/w40k_exporter.py. First adopted snapshot: data_version: 886.

Why§

Wahapedia was a scrape of a third-party site; the app export is a clean licensed source. The app carries current points directly, so the MFM overlay's reason to exist disappeared with it.

What changed§

  • Identity system: faction and datasheet ids switched from Wahapedia short codes and 9-digit ids to lowercase hex UUIDs. Legacy 9-digit ids are dead; any still found in user data are stale data to fix.
  • Chapters became first-class factions: Adeptus Astartes is a parent faction with Blood Angels, Dark Angels, Space Wolves and the rest as children via faction.parent_faction. The load-time chapter rollup was removed.
  • User data rewritten in place by scripts/migrate_to_app40k.py (idempotent, dry-run first, backup kept as collection.db.pre-app40k). The bsdata_id / unit_bsdata_id column names were kept as a legacy misnomer and now hold UUIDs.
  • Wahapedia CSVs archived under archive/data/wahapedia-2026-06/; the catalogue_* and mfm_* tables were dropped by scripts/cleanup_post_app40k.py.
  • virtual_bool is forced False for every datasheet; the new export has no equivalent semantic (accepted behavioural change).

entries.title: Ruleset source: BSData to Wahapedia
entries.summary: Replaced the BSData XML import with the Wahapedia CSV export as the sole ruleset source.
entries.html:
The original ruleset import, the community-maintained BSData wh40k-10e XML files, was replaced by the Wahapedia CSV export as the sole source of factions, datasheets and rules data.

What changed§

  • All rules data (factions, datasheets, weapon profiles, points) now loaded from Wahapedia's CSV export into the catalogue_* tables.
  • Current points came from a separate Munitorum Field Manual overlay (mfm_* tables) layered over the Wahapedia base data.
  • A pre-migration snapshot of user data was kept as collection.db.pre-wahapedia.

This is the earliest recorded source migration; detail beyond the above was not captured at the time. Both the BSData and Wahapedia pipelines have since been retired entirely (see the June 2026 migration to the official app's database).
